have h₁ : ∃ (a : α), a ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := by
      -- Since α is inhabited, it is non-empty, and the sample function's support is the entire type
      have h₂ : Nonempty α := ⟨default⟩
      have h₃ : default ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := SampleableType.mem_support_selectElem _
      exact ⟨default, h₃⟩
    have h₂ : ∃ (b : β), b ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := by
      -- Similarly for β

      have h₄ : default ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := SampleableType.mem_support_selectElem _
      exact ⟨default, h₄⟩
    -- We can now find a and b in the supports of α and β, respectively
    obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := h₁
    obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := h₂

        -- Use the fact that the product of supports is in the support of the product distribution

          -- Use the fact that the product of supports is in the support of the product distribution
          exact Set.mem_of_superset (Set.mem_range_self (a, b)) (by
            -- Use the fact that the product of supports is in the support of the product distribution

            simp only [Set.mem_range, Set.mem_range] at hp ⊢

        -- Use the fact that the product of supports is in the support of the product distribution

      -- Use the fact that the product of supports is in the support of the product distribution

    -- Since x is in the product type, it can be written as (a, b)

have h₁ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := SampleableType.mem_support_selectElem x.1
    have h₂ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := SampleableType.mem_support_selectElem x.2
    have h₃ : (x.1, x.2) ∈ support ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := by
      -- We need to show that (x.1, x.2) is in the support of (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)
      -- This follows directly from the fact that x.1 and x.2 are in the supports of $ᵗ α and $ᵗ β, respectively
      -- and the support of the product is the product of the supports
      have h₄ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := h₁
      have h₅ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := h₂
      have h₆ : (x.1, x.2) ∈ support ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the product of supports is the support of the product
        have h₇ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := h₁
        have h₈ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := h₂
        -- Since (x.1, x.2) is the result of applying (·, ·) to (x.1, x.2), it is in the support
        have h₉ : (·, ·) x.1 x.2 ∈ support ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := by
          -- Use the property of product distributions
          have h₁₀ : 0 < prob (x.1, x.2) ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := by
            -- Since x.1 and x.2 are in their respective supports, their product is in the product support
            calc
              0 < prob x.1 ($ᵗ α) * prob x.2 ($ᵗ β) := by
                -- Both prob x.1 ($ᵗ α) and prob x.2 ($ᵗ β) are positive
                have h₁₁ : 0 < prob x.1 ($ᵗ α) := by
                  have h₁₂ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := h₁
                  exact prob_pos_of_in_support h₁₂
                have h₁₃ : 0 < prob x.2 ($ᵗ β) := by
                  have h₁₄ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := h₂
                  exact prob_pos_of_in_support h₁₄
                positivity
              _ = prob (x.1, x.2) ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := by
                -- Calculate the probability of the product
                simp [prob_map, prob_fst_mul_prob_snd, Function.comp]
                <;> ring
                <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.density_mul]
                <;> field_simp [add_assoc]
                <;> ring_nf
                <;> simp_all [add_assoc]
                <;> linarith
          -- Since the probability is positive, (x.1, x.2) is in the support
          have h₁₁ : 0 < prob (x.1, x.2) ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := h₁₀
          have h₁₂ : (x.1, x.2) ∈ support ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) := by
            -- Use the property of supports
            exact mem_support_iff.mpr (fun h₁₃ => by
              -- If the probability were zero, it would contradict h₁₀
              have h₁₄ : prob (x.1, x.2) ((·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)) = 0 := by simpa using h₁₃
              linarith)
          -- Since (·, ·) x.1 x.2 = (x.1, x.2), we have (x.1, x.2) in the support
          simpa [Prod.mk.injEq] using h₁₂
        -- Therefore, (x.1, x.2) is in the support
        simpa [Prod.mk.injEq] using h₉
      -- Therefore, (x.1, x.2) is in the support
      simpa [Prod.mk.injEq] using h₆
    -- Therefore, x is in the support
    simpa [Prod.mk.injEq] using h₃
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by sorry
  probFailure_selectElem := by sorry

  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by
    have h₁ : Pr[= x | (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)] = Pr[= y | (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)] := by
      calc
        Pr[= x | (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)] = Pr[fun (a : α × β) => a = x | (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)] := rfl
        _ = (fun p => Pr[p | (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)]) (fun (a : α × β) => a = x) := rfl
        _ = (fun p => Pr[p | (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)]) (fun (a : α × β) => a = y) := by

intro z
    have h : z ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := by
      apply SampleableType.mem_support_selectElem
    exact h
  
  have h₃ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := by
    have h₃₁ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := h₁ x.1
    exact h₃₁
  
  have h₄ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := by
    have h₄₁ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := h₂ x.2
    exact h₄₁
  
  have h₅ : (x.1, x.2) ∈ support ((fun x1 x2 => (x1, x2)) <$> $ᵗ α <*> $ᵗ β) := by
    have h₅₁ : x.1 ∈ support ($ᵗ α) := h₃
    have h₅₂ : x.2 ∈ support ($ᵗ β) := h₄
    have h₅₃ : (x.1, x.2) ∈ support ((fun x1 x2 => (x1, x2)) <$> $ᵗ α <*> $ᵗ β) := by
